original_abstract: This paper explores the educational philosophy of Imam Al-Ghazali and evaluates its enduring relevance within the contemporary academic landscape. A preeminent scholar in Islamic tradition, Al-Ghazali argued that education should transcend the mere transfer of information, serving instead as a vital vehicle for character development and spiritual cultivation. Utilizing a library research methodology, this study examines his insights into moral and ethical instruction to determine how his classical views can address the challenges facing modern education. Al-Ghazali advocated for learning methods that are active, reflective, and rooted in spiritual values—approaches that offer a robust framework for strengthening ethics in today’s schools. Ultimately, this research seeks to bridge traditional Islamic thought with current educational needs, identifying the transformative contributions Al-Ghazali’s legacy can offer to the modern global education system.
humanized_abstract: This paper explores Imam Al-Ghazali’s educational philosophy and assesses its validity and relevance to contemporary education. According to Al-Ghazali, the Islamic scholar of the highest standing, education is not a transactional process wherein knowledge is communicated and students learn. Rather, it is a process that builds the human self and evolves the individual as a spiritually evolved person. Based on library research, this paper analyses Imam Al-Ghazali’s theory of moral and ethical education, and discusses how his classical perspective of education can possibly address the modern educational problems and challenges. In other words, his suggestions for skill based and value-orientated pedagogies for building strong ethics in schools can serve as a paradigm that addresses the societal needs of today’s time. This paper therefore attempts to explore the potential of reconciling ancient Islamic principles of education and contemporary education with the aim of discovering the possible positive effects of Al-Ghazali’s educational ideology on the modern global education system.
